nodes.label: ui backend fastapi
nodes.description: ui/backend/main.py — FastAPI app serving the React frontend. Re-exports the same lib.query/lib.audit functions the CLI and MCP use, plus orchestrates the asynchronous ingestion job that spawns a dedicated Claude session. Runs on port 8000 (default) with reload enabled. CORS is wide open for the dev environment.

nodes.label: backend read only default kuzu
nodes.description: _conn() in ui/backend/main.py opens kuzu.Database with read_only=True. Every endpoint reads from this connection. Rationale: lets the frontend issue many concurrent GETs while a CLI ingestion writes from another process; no risk of accidental writes through an exposed HTTP endpoint.

nodes.label: cors allow all dev choice
nodes.description: CORSMiddleware is configured with allow_origins=['*'], allow_methods=['GET','POST'], allow_headers=['*']. Intentional dev-mode posture — the frontend (Vite on :5173) and backend (FastAPI on :8000) run on different origins. Production deployment would tighten origins, but brAIn is a local-only tool so the loose default is acceptable.

nodes.label: safe cypher string strip
nodes.description: _safe(s) strips ['"\\\n\r] and truncates to 120 chars. Applied to ALL user-supplied string fragments inlined into Cypher (q in /api/search, node_type filter, node_id in /api/node). Mitigates Cypher injection through user input. Parameterized queries would be cleaner but Kuzu's `f-string`-style inline format is used here for the dynamic WHERE-clause construction.

nodes.label: endpoint stats
nodes.description: GET /api/stats — wraps lib.query.stats. Returns counts by node type and rel type, plus totals. Used by the header chips in App.jsx. On any exception, returns {error: str} instead of HTTP 500 so the UI degrades gracefully.

nodes.label: endpoint audit
nodes.description: GET /api/audit — wraps lib.audit.run_audit. Returns {metrics, warnings, errors}. Currently consumed only by introspection; the frontend does not surface audit results in the main UI yet.

nodes.label: endpoint graph
nodes.description: GET /api/graph — returns the WHOLE graph as {nodes, edges}, ordered by importance DESC. The frontend filters and lays it out client-side; the backend does not pre-filter. Acceptable today because the graph fits in memory; will need pagination once the graph grows past a few thousand nodes.

nodes.label: endpoint node detail
nodes.description: GET /api/node/{node_id}?neighbors=20 — wraps context_for_topic with limit=1, so it returns the first match (by importance) plus its top-20 outgoing/incoming edges by confidence. Returns {error: str} if no match instead of HTTP 404. The id is _safe-stripped before being substring-matched.

nodes.label: endpoint search
nodes.description: GET /api/search?q=&node_type=&limit=50 — substring match on label or description (case-insensitive), filtered by node_type if set, ordered by importance. limit caps at 200. Both q and node_type pass through _safe(). Drives the sidebar search box in App.jsx.

nodes.label: endpoint types list
nodes.description: GET /api/types — DISTINCT node types with counts. Drives the type-filter chips in the sidebar; ordered by count so the most-populated types appear first.

nodes.label: endpoint ingest post
nodes.description: POST /api/ingest {project_name, folder_path} — spawns a background thread that launches a dedicated Claude session to autonomously extract and ingest the project. Validates inputs: project_name stripped to alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen, folder_path must be an existing directory. Returns {job_id} immediately so the frontend can start polling.

nodes.label: endpoint ingest status
nodes.description: GET /api/ingest/{job_id} — returns the current job dict: status (running|done|error), output (accumulated stdout), pid, exit_code, project_name, folder_path. The frontend polls this every 2 seconds while the job is running.

nodes.label: ingest prompt template
nodes.description: INGEST_PROMPT_TEMPLATE in ui/backend/main.py — multi-step prompt fed to Claude when an ingestion job starts. Six steps: read SKILL.md, discover files, batch them by theme, apply the extraction protocol per batch, ingest each payload, verify with stats+audit, do gap verification per batch. The template uses placeholders BRAIN_ROOT / PROJECT_NAME / FOLDER_PATH replaced by .replace(). The prompt is the actual contract with the spawned Claude — Claude follows it autonomously; it's the same template that drove this very extraction.

nodes.label: claude session subprocess
nodes.description: _run_ingestion uses subprocess.Popen to launch `claude --print --dangerously-skip-permissions <prompt>` as a child process from the brAIn root. stdout is captured line by line and appended to job['output'] in real time. The flags: --print to use non-interactive mode, --dangerously-skip-permissions because the ingestion needs unattended file reads and ingest tool calls.

nodes.label: claude binary discovery
nodes.description: _find_claude() resolves the claude CLI by (a) shutil.which('claude') first, (b) falling back to ~/.local/bin/claude, /usr/local/bin/claude, /usr/bin/claude. Returns None if none found, which is surfaced as a job error ('claude CLI not found on PATH'). Tolerant of multiple install patterns.

nodes.label: in memory jobs dict
nodes.description: Module-level _jobs: dict[str, dict] keeps every job's state until process restart. Key is an 8-char uuid hex. No persistence — restarting the backend loses job history. Acceptable for a local tool; would need a database for multi-user or multi-instance deployments.

nodes.label: threading background ingest
nodes.description: start_ingest spawns threading.Thread(daemon=True). Daemon=True means the thread is torn down on backend shutdown without blocking. Each thread holds its own subprocess.Popen handle and writes into the shared _jobs dict. No lock — single thread per job, only the poll endpoint reads, which is acceptable because dict reads are atomic in CPython.

nodes.label: ingest input validation
nodes.description: project_name is regex-stripped to [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ then required non-empty (HTTP 400 otherwise). folder_path must be a non-empty string AND Path(folder_path).is_dir() must hold. The regex strip is intentionally permissive (only keeps safe filename chars) so the resulting project_name is safe to use as a path segment.

nodes.label: project dir auto creation
nodes.description: Before launching the Claude subprocess, the backend mkdir -p's projects/<project_name>/. Guarantees that the prompt's instruction to save JSON payloads into that directory will not fail with ENOENT. Idempotent — re-ingestions of the same project name reuse the directory.

nodes.label: graceful error envelope pattern
nodes.description: Several GET endpoints (stats, audit) catch ALL exceptions and return {error: str} as 200 instead of raising HTTP 5xx. Rationale: the UI polls these on mount; a 5xx would break the chrome (header, sidebar). Wrapping degrades the chip gracefully and surfaces the error message in the UI without blocking the rest of the page.

nodes.label: ui start sh script
nodes.description: ui/start.sh — convenience launcher that brings up both servers: `uvicorn ui.backend.main:app --reload --port 8000` and `npm run dev` (Vite on 5173) as background jobs, then `wait`s. A trap on EXIT kills both PIDs on Ctrl+C. Uses a relative ROOT resolved from $0 so it works wherever brAIn is cloned.

rels.evidence: Imports lib.db, lib.query, lib.audit and dispatches to the same functions the CLI uses.
Implemented in _conn() of ui/backend/main.py.
Read-only HTTP plus a writer-via-CLI lets the UI poll the graph while a separate Claude subprocess writes to it without lock contention.
CORSMiddleware configured at app construction.
_safe() applied at every user-input → Cypher boundary.
Search query and node_type stripped before inlining.
Node id stripped before substring matching.
/api/stats endpoint.
/api/audit endpoint.
/api/graph endpoint.
/api/node/{node_id} endpoint.
/api/search endpoint.
/api/types endpoint.
POST /api/ingest endpoint.
GET /api/ingest/{job_id} endpoint.
Wraps lib.query.stats.
Wraps lib.audit.run_audit.
Wraps context_for_topic with limit=1.

Endpoint /api/graph returns the WHOLE graph for client-side filtering; /api/search returns a server-filtered subset. The two coexist because one supports interactive exploration (graph) and the other supports targeted lookup (search) — but mixing them would be confusing.
POST /api/ingest spawns the Claude subprocess via _run_ingestion in a background thread.
Uses threading.Thread(daemon=True) to return immediately while the job runs.
Validation runs at the top of start_ingest.
Creates projects/<name>/ before launching the subprocess.
Inserts the new job dict before returning the job_id.
Looks up the job by id in _jobs.
The template is the prompt argument passed to claude --print.
The template literally instructs the spawned Claude to follow the SKILL.md extraction protocol.
_find_claude() must succeed before Popen is called.
/api/stats catches Exception and returns {error: str}.
/api/audit catches Exception and returns {error: str}.
Launches uvicorn against ui.backend.main:app.
Worker thread mutates the job dict to surface progress to the poll endpoint.
The substituted INGEST_PROMPT_TEMPLATE is the only argument the subprocess receives beyond flags.
